pattern: Grouping transactions by name turns the cross-city time-proximity check into a localized pairwise scan, while the amount rule is a simple per-transaction filter.
hints: Two independent reasons make a transaction suspect: a large amount, or a same-name transaction in another city close in time. Handle them separately then union the flagged ones.
Only transactions sharing a name can trigger the proximity rule, so bucket by name first; within a bucket compare times across differing cities.
Parse each record into name/time/amount/city, flag any amount over 1000, and flag both members of any same-name pair within 60 minutes whose cities differ.

edge_inputs.description: two same-name records in different cities within window
only the over-amount one is invalid
different names so proximity never applies
same city within window stays valid on amount alone
exactly 60 minutes apart, different cities, both flagged
61 minutes apart is outside the window
amount exactly 1000 is not over the limit

stress.description: many records over a small name and city pool to force collisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    names = ['al', 'bo', 'cy', 'da']
    cities = ['mtv', 'nyc', 'sf', 'la']
    txs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        nm = rng.choice(names)
        t = rng.randint(0, 200)
        amt = rng.randint(0, 2000)
        ct = rng.choice(cities)
        txs.append('%s,%d,%d,%s' % (nm, t, amt, ct))
    return [txs]
stress.note: tiny pools maximise same-name cross-city proximity collisions
